utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi jeremy how are you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm really good thank you how are you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm okay the the medical assistant told me that you had this ulcer on your foot that's been there for a couple of weeks

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: going away

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah it's been there gosh it's like six or so weeks right now and it's and it's on my right foot and it's just yeah it's just not going away i'm not sure if it maybe even gotten a little worse from when i first noticed it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and how long did you say it's going on for

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: probably about

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: six eight weeks maybe

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and do you have any pain in your foot

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no no no pain at all

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay now i know that you're a diabetic and you are on some insulin have your sugars been running okay yeah they have been running

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you know on the most part they seem to be running a little higher than normal

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: your sugars are running higher than normal

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you recall what your last hemoglobin a1c was was it above nine

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes it it it definitely was higher than nine

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright now what do you think caused this ulcer were you wearing some tight fitting shoes or did you have some trauma to your foot or

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i was you know i think initially i'm you know i was out in the backyard you know kind of you know doing some work and you know i know i you know i could've stepped on a nail or you know there was some other work but you know i'm always outside so i do n't know if that kind of led to anything or caused anything

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and have you had any fever or chills

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no no no fever or chills you know i kinda you know get headaches pretty often i do n't know if that you know i do n't know if that's a stress or but you know always have like the tension headaches in the front

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and do you have do you have neuropathy where you get like numbing and tingling in your feet

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: occasionally yeah occasionally especially when it's like colder outside

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm kinda feels like it takes a little longer to

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: warm up but yeah i kinda have some sensation in in all my extremities

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and then are you are you a smoker or did you smoke

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did back you know kind of years ago i did but yeah i have n't smoked anything in in good number of years

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright when did you stop smoking

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: couple years ago maybe four or so years ago

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and how many packs a day would you smoke

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: gosh back then yeah was at least two

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright how many years did you smoke for like twenty

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah at least twenty yeah twenty plus years

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright now any other symptoms do you have any problems when you walk down the street do you get any pain in your calves at all when you walk

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no no no no pain you know just kind of you know it's just i know that it's there

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and you said you're active you're out in the yard and things like that do you go on long walks at all or no

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no no you know it's you know i just kinda feel like i've been just trying to take it easy lately

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but yeah most most of the stuff i've been doing is just kind of hanging around the house

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright so we talked a little bit about your diabetes let's talk about your heart disease now your heart disease you had a heart attack in twenty eighteen we put a stent into your right coronary artery you're still taking your medications for that you're still on your aspirin

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i am yes yeah i do the baby aspirin every day

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and any chest pain or shortness of breath or anything like that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no no yeah no nothing more than yeah i would n't attribute anything

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and do you have a podiatrist for your yearly foot exams

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i i i do n't

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright alright well let's go ahead i wan na just do a quick physical exam i'm just gon na be calling out some of my exam findings so your vital signs here in the office you do n't have any fever so that's good your blood pressure is great it's like one twenty seven over eighty and your heart rate is nice and slow in the sixties on your neck exam i do n't appreciate any jugular venous distention or any carotid bruits on your lung exam your lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally on your heart exam you do have a two out of six systolic ejection murmur heard at the left base and on your lower extremity exam i do n't appreciate any palpable dorsalis pedis or posterior tibial pulses there is a two by three centimeter ulcerated lesion on the right lateral foot near the fifth metacarpal metatarsophalangeal joint there is no associated cellulitis does it hurt when i press here

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: there is no pain to palpation of the right foot there is associated granulation tissue and some slight purulent discharge from the wound okay so what does all that mean that just means that you have this ulcer that's you know fairly sizable with i think we need to do some good wound care on it let's talk a little bit about my assessment and plan so you know i you have a nonhealing ulcer of your right foot so we need to do some studies on you to see if you have an adequate blood supply to heal this foot wound and since you since you probably do n't because of your diabetes you're here in a vascular surgeon's office we may have to go ahead and talk about being able to open up some of your arteries to improve the blood supply to your foot so that might mean getting a stent to one of your arteries in your legs to open up the blood supply it might mean mean that we might have to do some bypass surgery to to improve the blood supply to your foot in order to heal that that wound i do think that you'll be able to heal it i do n't think that we need to do anything drastic i want you to continue with your aspirin because that will help

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any questions

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i mean is this do we have to do any more tests or anything what are you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: we're gon na do an arterial ultrasound i'm going to go ahead and order an arterial ultrasound of your lower extremities to see what the blood supply is like and then i'm gon na go ahead and order a podiatry consult because i want them to see this wound and improve the wound care that you're doing and then for your next problem your diabetes i wan na go ahead and talk to your primary care physician we need to get your diabetes better controlled because that impacts your wound healing as well okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure understood alright and for your last issue your coronary artery disease continue with your statin and i will talk to your cardiologist in case you need a procedure to see if you're cleared from a medical standpoint okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay perfect

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: perfect thank you so much

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay bye

detail_list.summary: Right foot ulcer
The patient reports a right foot ulcer present for 6 to 8 weeks that may have worsened.
The doctor asks about the duration of the foot ulcer.
The doctor asks if the patient has pain in the foot.
The patient denies pain in the foot.
Diabetes
The patient's blood sugar has been running higher than normal.
The doctor asks if the patient's last hemoglobin A1c was above 9.
The patient confirms his last hemoglobin A1c was higher than 9.
The doctor asks about potential causes of the ulcer such as trauma or footwear.
The patient reports he was working in the backyard and is unsure if he sustained trauma to the foot.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced fevers or chills.
The patient denies fevers or chills but reports frequent tension headaches.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences numbness and tingling in his feet.
The patient reports occasional numbness and tingling in his feet, especially in cold weather.
The doctor asks about the patient's smoking history.
The patient stopped smoking 4 years ago.
The patient smoked 2 packs a day.
The patient smoked for over 20 years.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences calf pain when walking.
The patient denies calf pain when walking.
The patient is not very active and mostly stays around the house.
Coronary artery disease
The patient takes aspirin 81 mg daily.
The doctor asks if the patient has chest pain or shortness of breath.
The patient denies chest pain or shortness of breath.
The doctor asks if the patient has a podiatrist for yearly foot exams.
The patient does not have a podiatrist.

Physical exam findings: BP 127/80, HR 60s, no JVD or carotid bruits, lungs clear, 2/6 systolic ejection murmur at left base, no palpable pedal pulses, 2x3 cm right lateral foot ulcer with granulation tissue and purulent discharge, no cellulitis, no pain to palpation.
The doctor explains the ulcer is non-healing and requires further studies to assess blood supply, potentially including stent placement or bypass surgery.
The doctor advises the patient to continue taking aspirin 81 mg daily.
The patient asks if further tests are required.
The doctor will order an arterial ultrasound of the lower extremities and a podiatry consult, and advises the patient to follow up with his primary care physician for diabetes management.
The doctor advises the patient to continue his statin and will contact the cardiologist for medical clearance.

case_description: Right foot ulcer; Diabetes;Coronary artery disease
